candidate: Sean stands in three-quarter view, body rotated approximately 45 degrees off-front toward screen-left, full body visible head-to-toe. Both eyes read — near eye in full, far eye foreshortened but present behind the bridge of the nose; far brow visible; the nose breaks the far-cheek silhouette slightly. Shoulders offset in depth (near shoulder forward, far shoulder receding), not stacked in profile. Right hand raised mid-gesture at chest height, palm angled toward camera, fingers relaxed and separable. Left hand relaxed at side. NOT a full profile — the far eye must not be hidden.
golden: left profile, full body visible

candidate: STRICT FRONT VIEW: head and shoulders square to camera, zero rotation, face symmetric about the vertical midline. Both eyes equal in width and both fully visible — neither foreshortened. Nose ridge centered on the facial midline; both ears equally visible (or equally hair-occluded) at the silhouette edges. Shoulder line horizontal, both shoulders the same depth. Not three-quarter. Not turned.
golden: three-quarter view

candidate: Screen-relative orientation: the character's body and face are turned so the camera sees the LEFT side of the head — nose, lips, and chin form the side silhouette along the LEFT edge of the frame; the back of the skull and nape sit toward the RIGHT edge. Do not mirror. Reference convention: screen-left = viewer's left half of the canvas.
golden: right profile

candidate: Camera positioned directly behind Sean. Render only the back of the head and body — back of the skull, the nape, the hair crown-to-nape flow with the center cowlick reading as the highest point of the silhouette, the rear of the navy tee, the rear jeans pockets, the back of the heels. The head is NOT rotated toward the camera; head and shoulders share the same back-facing axis. No facial features visible from this angle: no eye, no eyebrow, no nose, no mouth, no ear, no jaw line, no cheek.
golden: three-quarter back angle, full body visible

candidate: Sean in a head-and-shoulders portrait, three-quarter view — body rotated approximately 45 degrees off front so both eyes read clearly, with the far eye foreshortened but fully visible behind the bridge of the nose and the far brow line still readable. The far cheek silhouette extends past the nose, not occluded by it. Pencil-test register: soft graphite line, light color wash, cross-hatch shadow, warm cream paper.
golden: turned about sixty degrees from camera, between a three-quarter view and a profile

candidate: Each hand has exactly five digits — one thumb plus four fingers, never six. Left hand on the keyboard: thumb resting at the near edge of the home row, four fingers (index, middle, ring, pinky) spread across the keys — count the four keytop fingertips before committing the contour, do not insert a sixth digit between index and middle. Right hand on the mouse: thumb wrapping the near side, index and middle resting on top of the mouse body, ring and pinky curled at the far side, five total. Render finger separation cleanly with warm-graphite contour so the count is verifiable.
golden: with five fingers on each hand

candidate: Raised right hand at chest height, palm angled toward camera, fingers clearly separated and individually drawn — five distinct digits (thumb wrapping toward the viewer, four fingers spread with visible gaps between each, knuckles articulated). Each finger rendered with its own contour line in warm graphite #3D3530, with cross-hatched shadow in the gaps between fingers and along the underside of the palm.
